15.reason: The format string "%d " expects an extra space after the integer, which may cause input mismatch or skip next input. Should be "%d".

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  The format string "%d " expects an extra space after the integer, which may cause input mismatch or skip next input. Should be "%d".
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  The trailing space in scanf format causes the program to block waiting for non-whitespace after reading the integer, leading to incorrect input synchronization.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Trailing space in scanf format may cause the program to wait for additional non-whitespace input, leading to indefinite blocking when input ends. Also, this reads before the overflow check, but the check is above.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  The trailing space in the format string causes scanf to consume all trailing whitespace and wait for non-whitespace input, breaking normal input parsing.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [The space after %d in scanf can cause input parsing issues, especially when next input is immediately following without whitespace]

21.reason: After printing "error " for a full stack, incrementing i makes i=101, which breaks the stack size logic and causes subsequent operations to misbehave.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.25 , reason:  After printing error for full stack, incrementing i makes it 101, which breaks the stack size logic and causes subsequent operations to misbehave.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  After printing "error " for a full stack, incrementing i makes i=101, which breaks the stack size logic and causes subsequent operations to misbehave.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  After a push on a full stack, i is incremented to 101, corrupting the stack pointer and causing subsequent push operations to be ignored without error.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Incrementing the stack pointer i on a push error corrupts the stack state; i should remain unchanged when the stack is full.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  Incrementing the stack index when the stack is full corrupts the pointer; it should remain unchanged to correctly reflect the full state.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [Incrementing i beyond 100 leads to invalid stack state; should not increment on error]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Incrementing i beyond 100 leads to invalid stack state; once full, further pushes should not alter i.]
